mappings.rationaleAI: Comprehensive wave treatment addresses general phenomena (A), electromagnetic waves (B), light waves (C), and sound (E).
Sound physics addresses nature of sound waves (E.1), shock waves (E.2), sources (E.3), reception (E.4), applications including recording (E.5.a) and noise control (E.5.e), and musical sound (E.6).
Light physics addresses wave theory (C.1), speed of light (C.2), lasers (B.4), quantum theory of light (C.8), and focusing and imaging (D).
Wave-particle duality addresses quantum theory of light (C.8), photon phenomena (C.8.a), and uncertainty principle (C.8.b).
Maxwell's unification connects to electromagnetic wave properties (B.1).
Telescope design covers electromagnetic spectrum (B.2), optics (D.1), and optical systems (D.1.b).
Ionizing radiation connects to X-rays (B.2.f), gamma rays (B.2.g), X-ray sources (B.3.d), and gamma sources (B.3.e).
X-ray diffraction connects to wave interference (A.6) and X-rays (B.2.f).
Speed of light as cosmic limit relates to velocity of light measurement (C.2).
Light and electron microscopy connect to electromagnetic spectrum (B.2), X-rays (B.2.f), and geometrical optics (D.1).
Fundamental physics connects broadly to wave phenomena (A), electromagnetic waves (B), and light (C).
Seismic waves connect to wave classification (A.2.a), boundary behavior (A.5), and shock waves (E.2).
